title: Pan-Seared Pork Gyoza with Melty Cheese
description: A fusion twist on traditional Japanese gyoza, this pan-seared dish features juicy pork and a melty cheesy center.

ingredients.name: Melty Cheese
ingredients.notes: or other melty cheese of choice

instructions: Preheat a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add 1 tablespoon of sesame oil to the pan and swirl to coat.
Add the ground pork to the pan, breaking it up with a spoon as it cooks.
Once the pork is browned, add 1 tablespoon of soy sauce and stir to combine.
Lay a gyoza wrapper on a flat surface and place a tablespoon of the pork mixture in the center.
Place a small piece of melty cheese on top of the pork mixture.
Fold the wrapper over the filling to form a half-moon shape, pressing the edges to seal.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling.
Add the gyoza to the pan, leaving a small amount of space between each dumpling.
Pan-fry the gyoza until the bottoms are golden brown, about 2-3 minutes.
Flip the gyoza over and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the other side is also golden brown.
Serve the gyoza hot with additional soy sauce and green onions for garnish.
